human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1622 acceptance criteria, refreshed manifest, constrained research summary, functionality map, 20-scenario suite, evaluation results, deployment notes, commercialization notes, specification-based runtime posture, and refusal boundaries.

source_classes_covered: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation where applicable

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to secure software development advisory, secure design, secure implementation, code review, dependency, secret-handling, verification, remediation, release evidence, and documentation work.

Hard refusals cover cloud platform operations, CI/CD provider administration, network hardening, production incident remediation, legal/compliance/audit interpretation, data residency and tenant compliance policy, product strategy, unauthorized record mutation, release approval, risk acceptance, and secret handling.

Authority sources cover NIST SSDF, NIST CSF, CISA Secure by Design, OWASP SAMM, OWASP ASVS, MITRE CWE, SLSA, OpenSSF Scorecard, NARA records management, GitHub secure use/code scanning/dependency review, Node.js security, npm audit, RFC 9110, and current package baseline context.

Evaluation scenarios cover all required output shapes, normal in-scope design and implementation guidance, maintainability and correctness review, dependency remediation, secret-handling confidentiality, evidence packaging, missing evidence, conflicting policies, unauthorized record changes, adjacent routing, low-confidence escalation, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim a retained trusted built implementation without separate frequent-use promotion evidence.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by issue #1622.
Research output records source authority, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario-family coverage, and non-blocking unresolved ambiguity.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated spec pack for secure software development advisory and evidence work, not as a built execution runtime.
Runtime may remain specification-based unless a separate trusted-build promotion proves frequent use, tenant isolation, execution safety, rollback controls, and sustained validation performance.
